title: Wave-0: fix commented-out PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW production guard + vitest regression test
acceptance_criteria: In route.ts, implement the production guard at the TOP of the `if (process.env.PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW === 'true')` block: when process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production', log an error via src/lib/logger.ts and return NextResponse.json with HTTP 404 (D7 — same shape/status as the existing not-enabled 404 at ~L81; do NOT leak that the bypass exists).
The guard returns BEFORE any getCurrentUser() call (~L49) and BEFORE any prisma.user.update (~L58) in that block — verify by code position, not just behaviour.
Remove the now-redundant commented-out guard lines (41-47); no // dead-code left behind.

vitest test: with NODE_ENV='production' and PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW='true', POST returns status 404 AND mocked getCurrentUser was NOT called AND mocked prisma.user.update was NOT called AND the response body contains no tier/sessionId/url payload.
vitest test: with NODE_ENV !== 'production' and PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW='true', the dev bypass still works (regression guard — tier update happens, returns sessionId 'test_flow').
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run test all pass.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of the guard placement and the 404-vs-500 rationale
